channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Supreme Court Clarifies: Electoral Roll Deletion Doesn't Affect Citizenship
item.description: The Supreme Court of India clarified that removing a name from an electoral roll does not automatically strip a person of their citizenship. This ruling came while addressing a petition regarding over...

item.title: Disputed Cosmo City Property Dispute Involves Zimbabwean Woman and ActionSA Raid
item.description: A Zimbabwean woman allegedly occupying a double-storey house in Cosmo City locked herself inside the property during an ActionSA raid targeting alleged hijacked RDP houses. The dispute centers on inhe...

item.title: Quebec man granted new trial 32 years after murder conviction
item.description: Daniel Jolivet, a 69-year-old Quebec man, has been granted a new trial 32 years after his murder conviction. Convicted in 1994 for the deaths of four individuals in 1992, Jolivet was released from pri...

item.title: U.S. Oil Prices Climb Above $80 Per Barrel Amid Global Demand Shifts
item.description: U.S. oil prices have surged back above $80 per barrel, driven by increased global demand and geopolitical tensions in key oil-producing regions. The recent climb follows a period of volatility as mark...

item.title: Boarding school licence revoked amid allegations of past abuse by Paris Hilton
item.description: A private boarding school has had its operating licence revoked following allegations that it failed to protect students from sexual abuse, including claims made by celebrity Paris Hilton. The decisio...

item.title: Kenya's Diaspora Remittances Drop by KSh 2.4 Billion in June 2026
item.description: Kenya's remittance inflows fell by 4.7% in June 2026, dropping to KSh 48.6 billion from KSh 51 billion in May 2026. The Central Bank of Kenya noted that this decline is mainly due to reduced inflows f...

item.title: Czech Climber Dies After Fall During Masherbrum Ascent
item.description: A Czech mountaineer, Mr Bansky Jaroslav, died after falling from a cliff near Camp 1 on the 7,821-meter Masherbrum peak in Pakistan's Karakoram Range. The incident occurred during the summer climbing ...

item.title: Young Filipino Footballers Compete in Norway Cup 2026 with Robinsons Land Support
item.description: Young Filipino footballers from the Leyte Association of Organized Sports Football Club (LAOS FC) are set to participate in the Norway Cup 2026, the world's largest youth soccer tournament. This initi...

item.title: Chinese Researchers Develop High-Durability Flowing Zinc Slurry Battery for Renewable Energy Storage
item.description: Chinese researchers have developed a groundbreaking flowing zinc slurry battery capable of retaining 81% of its capacity after 5,500 charge-discharge cycles. This innovation, created by teams at Fudan...

item.title: Harold Ogedegbe's Platform Simplifies Study Abroad Guidance for Africans
item.description: Harold Ogedegbe, founder of GrandRoyal Travel and GrandRoyal Visa AI, is addressing the challenges Africans face when studying abroad. His platform simplifies the visa application and relocation proce...

item.title: Andy Burnham's Challenges as UK Prime Minister: Lessons from Past Leaders
item.description: The article examines Andy Burnham's transition to the UK prime ministership, emphasizing the need to avoid pitfalls faced by predecessors like Tony Blair and Gordon Brown. It highlights the importance...

item.title: Ghana TikToker Get One-Year Jail Term Fo False Claims About President's Ritual
item.description: A Ghanaian TikToker, Camilla Alhassan, has been sentenced to one year in prison after admitting to spreading false claims that President John Mahama buried 32 cows as part of a ritual to win the 2024 ...

item.title: Gachagua's Victory in Ol Kalou By-Election Sparks Political Shift in Kenya
item.description: The article details the results of the recent by-election in Ol Kalou, where Rigathi Gachagua's campaign secured an overwhelming 85% victory. This outcome highlights the effectiveness of his grassroot...

item.title: News Corp Australia Blocks Crawler Bots: Steps to Resolve Access Issues
item.description: The article explains that News Corp Australia has implemented software to manage crawler bot traffic on its websites. If users encounter access issues and are not bots, they are advised to temporarily...

item.title: 2026 Could Exceed 1.5°C Warming Threshold, Scientists Warn
item.description: The article discusses the potential for 2026 to be the warmest year on record, with scientists highlighting the increasing likelihood of crossing the 1.5°C global warming threshold. It notes that the ...

item.title: City of Cape Town to Provide Services at Gugulethu Civic Hall Next Week
item.description: The City of Cape Town will set up its 'Basket of Services' team at the Gugulethu Civic Hall (NY50) on July 22 to offer residents direct access to municipal support. This initiative, led by Mayoral Com...

item.title: Taiwan extends visa-free entry for Filipinos in reciprocal agreement
item.description: The article discusses the extension of visa-free entry for Filipino passport holders to Taiwan, which is part of a reciprocal agreement between the two regions. This decision was made independently of...

item.title: Shared Experiences: Stories of Dating a Friend's Ex Revealed
item.description: The article explores the complex and often emotionally charged experiences of individuals who have dated a friend's former partner, or vice versa. Through personal anecdotes shared by Guardian readers...

item.title: Supreme Court confirms final forfeiture of Emefiele's properties worth $2m
item.description: The Supreme Court has confirmed the final and permanent forfeiture of seven landed properties valued at $2,045,000 and share certificates linked to former Central Bank of Nigeria Governor Godwin Emefi...

item.title: Artist Transplants Iconic NYC Landmarks to Surreal Environments
item.description: In the series 'Misplaced New York,' artist Anton Repponen reimagines iconic New York City landmarks by relocating them to desolate, otherworldly settings. The Chrysler Building, Guggenheim Museum, and...
